svg.text: Tank-centered, symmetric field plans tuned for the current Green Crate farmer footprint: 21×21 farm actions, 25×25 vacuum pickup.
Water Tank buried below center level. Keeps tractor lanes clear.
VacuFarm chest. Needs hoe + seeds/fertilizer stored locally.
Filter head feeding seeds/fertilizer into the farmer.
Recommended conveyor trunk. Keep at least one output side touching each farmer.
Most minimal layout. Highest simplicity, lowest farmer count, most unused irrigated border.
Use when you want one simple centered VacuFarm core inside a 25×25 irrigated square.
Best 2-farmer compromise for a square field. Symmetric around the buried tank center.
Best balanced 2-farmer option: mirrored pair with one shared south trunk.
Recommended for the wiki-style cross field. Each quadrant gets one farmer placed 1 tile inward.
Most symmetric match for the wiki cross layout: 4 farmers, 4 filter heads, shared center trunk.
1. Bury the Water Tank below the field center. The sprinkler code only checks x/y range, so height does not break coverage.
2. A farmer should touch a black filter crate on one side and a conveyor/output side on another side. Do not trap it fully off-network.
3. For cross fields, place each farmer 1 tile inward from the inner quadrant corner. This wastes fewer action tiles than planting directly on the path edge.
4. If you want the cleanest visual throughput, merge the four farmer outputs into one southbound trunk after the center path, not before it.
5. Current farmer geometry: 21×21 work area, 25×25 pickup area. That means irrigation can be larger than active till/plant coverage without losing harvest pickup.
